title: Carex cheesemanniana
intro: Carex cheesemanniana, also known as Cheeseman's sedge, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae, characterized by its compact growth habit and delicate, golden-yellow foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Carex cheesemanniana include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Carex cheesemanniana is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Carex cheesemanniana is a species within the sedge family (Cyperaceae). This plant is classified as a member of the genus Carex, which consists of approximately 1500 species. The Carex cheesemanniana is native to the subtropical regions of New Zealand and is often found in wetland environments. The species is also known by the common names Cheeseman's sedge or Cheeseman's tussock sedge. Carex cheesemanniana is classified as a perennial plant, meaning it grows and regenerates year after year.
